title: achoriphragma sauricum
intro: Achoriphragma sauricum, also known as Dyer's woad, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is a flowering plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, known for its distinctive blue-violet flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Achoriphragma sauricum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried extracts for medicinal purposes, and its health benefits include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing.
